# ARLO DAW: Ableton — Prompt Rows

62 prompt rows for the DAW: Ableton stream. Each row produces one corpus file under `corpus/daw/ableton/{area}/`. The Master Template at the top of `PROMPTS.md` applies — substitute the variables and fire. The version-specific anti-hallucination notes from `DAW-ABLETON-RESEARCH-PLAN.md` apply to every row.

**Provenance note:** Files generated by these prompts will be tagged `Deep-research synthesis (2026-05) — Ableton Live 12.x` so that future re-verification passes can spot them when Live 13 ships.

---

## Stream-specific master template addendum

Every prompt in this stream operates under the standard ARLO master template (see `PROMPTS.md`) with these additional clauses inserted into the **Content requirements** block:

> **Live version targeting.** The target version is the current shipping Ableton Live (Live 12.x as of 2026-05). When a feature, device, or workflow you cite was added in a specific Live version, name the version inline (e.g., "Hybrid Reverb (Live 11+)", "the Envelope Follower modulator (Live 12+)"). If a tutorial source predates Live 12 and you cannot verify the technique still works as described, OMIT it — don't retrofit older claims into Live 12 wording.
>
> **Source preference for this stream.** Prefer Ableton's official Live 12 Reference Manual and Release Notes as the primary citation. Supplement with Ableton Learn Live videos (timestamped), Sound on Sound Ableton columns, and version-stamped creator tutorials (Mad Zach, Slynk, Mr. Bill, Tom Cosm, Ill Gates, Andrew Huang, Ableton Certified Trainers). Use forum posts only as triangulation hints — never as the cited source. Refuse to cite AI-generated tutorial sites.
>
> **Cross-link the existing corpus.** Before writing, search `corpus/` for files already covering the DAW-agnostic concept. If found, end the file with a `See also:` line listing the relative paths. Examples: `corpus/sampling/chopping-resampling-and-warping.md`, `corpus/synthesis/subtractive-synthesis-fundamentals.md`, `corpus/rhythm/groove-construction-and-pocket.md`.
>
> **Default tags.** Every file gets `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, plus the area tag and topic-specific tags listed per row.
>
> **Platform target — macOS only.** This stream targets macOS Live 12.x. Where keyboard shortcuts, paths, or OS-specific friction differ between macOS and Windows, give the macOS form only. Do not include Windows-only material. (A future Windows-parity pass can branch from these files later.)
>
> **Max for Live boundary.** Do not cover Max for Live device-building, the Live Object Model (LOM), or M4L scripting. However, **factory M4L devices that ship bundled with Live and behave as built-in** (e.g., Convolution Reverb Pro, the LFO MIDI device, the Envelope MIDI device, the older Granulator, Pitch Hack, Buffer Shuffler) are in scope when they're idiomatic to a workflow you're describing. Cite them with their bundled-pack name (e.g., "Max for Live Essentials → Convolution Reverb Pro") so the reader knows where to find them.

---

## A. Fundamental Workflows — `corpus/daw/ableton/workflows/`

#### A1. `session-vs-arrangement-view`

- **Title:** Session View vs Arrangement View — When to Use Each
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/workflows
- **Subtopics:**
  - the two views' purposes (Session = nonlinear/loop-based, Arrangement = timeline)
  - the canonical Session-to-Arrangement workflow (record session play into arrangement)
  - the Arrangement-to-Session direction (rare but possible, what breaks)
  - Live 12 enhancements to view-switching (Capture, Bounce-to-New-Track interactions)
  - when producers stay in Session view for an entire project vs. flip to Arrangement
  - the role of Scenes as a song-form scaffold
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Session View, Arrangement View, Recording Session Play into Arrangement; Ableton Learn Live videos on Session/Arrangement workflow; Tom Cosm tutorials (version-stamped)
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `workflow`, `session-view`, `arrangement-view`, `methodology`

#### A2. `track-types-audio-midi-return-group`

- **Title:** Track Types in Live — Audio, MIDI, Return, Group
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/workflows
- **Subtopics:**
  - what each track type can and can't do (signal flow, monitoring, freezing)
  - Group tracks vs. Group Tracks 2.0 (Live 11+ nested groups)
  - Return tracks as the parallel-bus architecture
  - MIDI track output options (Audio From / To routing across tracks)
  - common gotchas (Return tracks can't be sidechain destinations, Group track latency)
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Tracks; Sound on Sound Live workflow articles
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `workflow`, `track-types`, `routing`, `principle`

#### A3. `routing-input-output-sends-sidechain`

- **Title:** Routing in Ableton Live — Input, Output, Sends, Sidechain
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/workflows
- **Subtopics:**
  - the four routing menus (Audio From, Monitor, Audio To, MIDI From/To)
  - "From" routing — pulling audio off another track post or pre-FX
  - send-and-return architecture
  - sidechain routing (Audio From a track into Compressor sidechain)
  - external routing (hardware inserts, ReWire, IAC bus for inter-app)
  - Live 12 routing changes (if any verified)
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Routing and I/O; SOS Inside Track Ableton features
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `workflow`, `routing`, `sidechain`, `principle`

#### A4. `recording-workflows-loop-comp`

- **Title:** Recording in Live — One-Take, Loop Record, and Comping
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/workflows
- **Subtopics:**
  - one-take recording (Session vs. Arrangement)
  - loop-recording into Session slots
  - Live 11+ takes-lane comping workflow
  - punch in/out conventions in Arrangement
  - the "record from playback" trick (re-record outputs into a new audio track)
  - automation recording modes (Latch, Touch, Re-Enable, Off)
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Recording; Ableton Learn Live comping video; MusicRadar Live 11 comping tutorials
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `workflow`, `recording`, `comping`, `automation`, `recipe`

#### A5. `clip-launching-and-follow-actions`

- **Title:** Clip Launching and Follow Actions
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/workflows
- **Subtopics:**
  - clip launch modes (Trigger, Gate, Toggle, Repeat)
  - launch quantization
  - the Follow Action grid (Stop, Play Again, Play Other, First, Last, Any, Other, Jump)
  - Live 11+ Follow Action chance and time enhancements
  - generative arrangement via Follow Actions
  - performance use of clip launching with a controller
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Launching Clips, Follow Actions; Ableton Learn Live Follow Actions video; Mr. Bill / Mad Zach generative tutorials
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `workflow`, `clip-launching`, `follow-actions`, `generative`, `performance`

#### A6. `freeze-flatten-consolidate-bounce`

- **Title:** Freeze, Flatten, Consolidate, and Bounce in Place
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/workflows
- **Subtopics:**
  - what Freeze actually does (renders the track output to a temporary file, disables CPU-heavy devices)
  - Flatten — replaces frozen track with its rendered audio (irreversible)
  - Consolidate — merges multiple clips on one track into a single audio clip
  - Bounce in Place (Live 12) — renders one or more tracks to new audio tracks, preserves source
  - which one to reach for in which situation (CPU pressure, commit-decision, sample-flip workflow)
  - what gets preserved vs. baked (effects, automation, sends)
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Freezing Tracks, Bounce to Audio, Consolidate; Ableton Help Center "Committing Audio in Live"
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `workflow`, `freeze`, `flatten`, `bounce`, `cpu`, `recipe`

#### A7. `instrument-racks-effect-racks-macros`

- **Title:** Instrument Racks, Effect Racks, and Macro Mapping
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/workflows
- **Subtopics:**
  - what a Rack is (parallel chains of devices with a shared macro interface)
  - the eight Macros and how to map them to multiple parameters
  - macro min/max range and curve
  - chain selectors and chain-zone modulation
  - key zones and velocity zones for layered instruments
  - MIDI-mapping macros to a hardware controller
  - the Live 12 macro count expansion (verify against manual)
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Instrument, Drum and Effect Racks; Ableton Learn Live Racks videos
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `workflow`, `racks`, `macros`, `chain-selector`, `principle`

## B. Stock Devices — `corpus/daw/ableton/devices/`

#### B1. `eq-eight-and-eq-controls`

- **Title:** EQ Eight (and Channel EQ) in Depth
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/devices
- **Subtopics:**
  - the eight bands and their filter-type options
  - Low/High/Bell/Notch/Shelf modes and Q behavior
  - Hi-Quality mode CPU vs. phase trade-off
  - Adaptive Q (Hi-Quality on)
  - oversampling and the analog-modeled mode
  - mid/side EQ on EQ Eight
  - **Channel EQ (Live 10+)** as the simpler/cheaper stock alternative — the originally-planned "EQ Controls" device does not exist
  - when to reach for EQ Eight vs. Channel EQ vs. third-party
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — EQ Eight; SOS EQ-in-Live articles
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `device`, `eq`, `eq-eight`, `mixing`

#### B2. `compressors-glue-multiband-drumbus`

- **Title:** Ableton's Compressors — Compressor, Glue, Multiband, Drum Buss
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/devices
- **Subtopics:**
  - Compressor (Peak vs. RMS detector, the three model curves)
  - Glue Compressor (SSL-style bus comp)
  - Multiband Dynamics (compress/expand per band)
  - Drum Buss (one-knob drum-bus device — drive, transients, boom)
  - which compressor to put on which source (drum bus → Glue or Drum Buss; vocals → Compressor; mix bus → Glue)
  - sidechain input on each
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Compressor, Glue Compressor, Multiband Dynamics, Drum Buss; Mr. Bill / Slynk drum-bus tutorials
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `device`, `compression`, `glue-compressor`, `drum-buss`, `mixing`

#### B3. `reverbs-classic-and-hybrid-reverb`

- **Title:** Reverbs in Live — Classic Reverb vs Hybrid Reverb
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/devices
- **Subtopics:**
  - the original Reverb device — algorithmic, with separate early and late stages
  - Hybrid Reverb (Live 11+) — combines convolution IR with algorithmic tail
  - choosing IRs in Hybrid Reverb (the bundled library, importing user IRs)
  - the **continuous Vintage degradation parameter** (Off → Extreme) — NOT "Vintage/Modern modes" as a chooser
  - the five algorithmic algorithms: Dark Hall, Quartz, Shimmer, Tides, Prism
  - when to reach for the classic Reverb (CPU-light, predictable) vs. Hybrid (character, IRs)
  - pre-delay, decay, freeze, and modulation controls
  - the bundled M4L Convolution Reverb Pro (Max for Live Essentials) as an alternative
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Reverb, Hybrid Reverb; Ableton Hybrid Reverb deep-dive video
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `device`, `reverb`, `hybrid-reverb`, `mixing`

#### B4. `unified-delay-device`

- **Title:** The Unified Delay Device (Live 10.1+)
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/devices
- **Subtopics:**
  - how the Live 10.1+ (May 2019) unified Delay replaced Simple Delay and Ping Pong Delay
  - the **three modes** — Repitch, Fade, Jump — and what each sounds like (the originally-planned fourth "Insert" mode does not exist)
  - sync vs. free-running time, dotted/triplet ratios
  - the Filter section (HP/LP within the feedback path)
  - Freeze, Ducking, Modulation
  - tape-style emulation with Repitch + Modulation
  - the older legacy Delay devices and Echo — when (if ever) they still make sense
- **Live version:** Live 12.x (Delay itself: Live 10.1+)
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Delay; Ableton Live 10.1 release notes on Delay unification
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `device`, `delay`, `mixing`

#### B5. `utility-modulation-autopan-autofilter-autoshift-gate`

- **Title:** The Utility Family — Utility, Auto Pan, Auto Filter, Auto Shift, Gate
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/devices
- **Subtopics:**
  - Utility (gain, polarity, mono-below, mid/side balance, width)
  - why Utility belongs almost everywhere in a Live signal chain
  - Auto Pan (LFO-driven panning and tremolo)
  - Auto Filter (LFO-driven filter with sidechain envelope follower)
  - Auto Shift (**Live 12.1+** — pitch and formant shifting; not in 12.0)
  - Gate (threshold, range, attack/hold/release, sidechain input)
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Utility, Auto Pan, Auto Filter, Auto Shift, Gate
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `device`, `utility`, `auto-pan`, `auto-filter`, `auto-shift`, `gate`, `mixing`

#### B6. `saturator-vs-roar`

- **Title:** Saturator vs Roar — Which to Reach For
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/devices
- **Subtopics:**
  - Saturator (classic, the tube/clip/digital/etc. curves, drive, DC offset, color)
  - Roar (Live 12 new — multi-stage saturation/distortion with routing)
  - Roar's stage modes (Tube, Digital Clipper, Diode Clipper, Wavetable, etc. — verify list)
  - parallel vs. series Roar configurations
  - when to reach for Saturator (transparent warmth, drum bus) vs. Roar (character, sound design, vocal distortion)
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Saturator, Roar; Ableton Roar walkthrough video
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `device`, `saturation`, `distortion`, `roar`, `mixing`

#### B7. `pedal-amp-cabinet`

- **Title:** Pedal, Amp, and Cabinet — Live's Guitar Color Chain
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/devices
- **Subtopics:**
  - Pedal device (Overdrive, Distortion, Fuzz modes)
  - Amp device (the seven amp models)
  - Cabinet device (the cab/mic options)
  - the canonical Pedal → Amp → Cabinet chain
  - using these on non-guitar sources (synth, drum bus, vocal)
  - IR-based alternatives (third-party impulse responses)
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Pedal, Amp, Cabinet
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `device`, `pedal`, `amp`, `cabinet`, `guitar`, `reamping`

#### B8. `spectral-resonator-spectral-time-pitch-time-machine`

- **Title:** Spectral and Pitch Devices — Spectral Resonator, Spectral Time, Pitch, Time Machine
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/devices
- **Subtopics:**
  - Spectral Resonator (Live 11+) — frequency-domain resonator, MIDI-pitched
  - Spectral Time (Live 11+) — freeze and time-blur in the spectral domain
  - Pitch — simple MIDI pitch-shifter device
  - Time Machine devices (Frequency Shifter, Ring Modulator) for sound design
  - when each fits (pitched resonance vs. spectral freeze vs. pitch-shift vs. clangy modulation)
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Spectral Resonator, Spectral Time, Pitch, Frequency Shifter, Ring Modulator
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `device`, `spectral-resonator`, `spectral-time`, `sound-design`

#### B9. `operator-analog-synths`

- **Title:** Operator and Analog — Live's Foundational Synths
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/devices
- **Subtopics:**
  - Operator (4-operator FM, with subtractive filter and waveform set)
  - the 11 Operator algorithms
  - additive mode on each operator
  - Analog (two-oscillator subtractive, twin filters, FM/sync)
  - patch-design starting points (bass, lead, pad, pluck) for each
  - when Operator vs. Analog vs. Wavetable (covered in B10)
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Operator, Analog; Sound on Sound *Synth Secrets* Operator articles
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `device`, `operator`, `analog`, `fm`, `subtractive`, `synthesis`

#### B10. `wavetable-drift-meld-synths`

- **Title:** Wavetable, Drift, and Meld — Live's Modern Synth Family
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/devices
- **Subtopics:**
  - Wavetable (Live 10+) — dual-oscillator, wavetable + MPE
  - Drift (**Live 11.3+, bundled with Live 12 Suite as standard**) — small, monophonic-friendly subtractive synth
  - Meld (Live 12+) — MPE-first dual-oscillator with macro-style controls
  - when to reach for each
  - MPE workflow on Wavetable and Meld
  - patch-design starting points per synth
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Wavetable, Drift, Meld; Ableton Drift/Meld walkthrough videos
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `device`, `wavetable`, `drift`, `meld`, `mpe`, `synthesis`

#### B11. `sampler-simpler-drum-rack-granulator-3`

- **Title:** Sampler, Simpler, Drum Rack, and Granulator III
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/devices
- **Subtopics:**
  - Simpler — the lightweight sampler, Classic / 1-Shot / Slice modes
  - Sampler — full multi-sample instrument, zones, modulation matrix
  - Drum Rack — 16-pad sampler with chain-per-pad processing
  - Granulator III (Live 12) — granular synthesis instrument
  - when each fits (Simpler for chops, Sampler for multi-sample instruments, Drum Rack for kits, Granulator for texture)
  - cross-link to E4 Slicing Audio to MIDI
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Simpler, Sampler, Drum Rack, Granulator III; Mad Zach Drum Rack tutorials (version-stamped)
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `device`, `sampler`, `simpler`, `drum-rack`, `granulator`, `sampling`

## C. Live 12 Features — `corpus/daw/ableton/live-12/`

#### C1. `modulators-shaper-lfo-envelope-follower-note-modulator`

- **Title:** Live 12 Modulators — Shaper, LFO, Envelope Follower (+ MIDI modulation family)
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/live-12
- **Subtopics:**
  - what a Modulator device is (lives on a track, modulates any mapped parameter)
  - Shaper — drawable modulation envelope (Live 12+)
  - LFO — tempo-syncable low-frequency oscillator (Live 12+)
  - Envelope Follower — drives modulation from an audio source (Live 12+)
  - **the MIDI-triggered modulation family** (NOT a "Note Modulator" — that device does not exist): Envelope MIDI, Shaper MIDI, Expression Control, MPE Control
  - the mapping workflow (right-click parameter → Map)
  - range, depth, polarity per mapping; bipolar vs unipolar
  - the biggest workflow shift in Live 12 — supersedes many older Max-for-Live LFO workarounds
  - **Filename note:** the file keeps the legacy "note-modulator" stub in its name for retrieval continuity, but the body explicitly debunks the claim that a Note Modulator device exists.
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Modulators; Ableton Modulators introduction video; Mr. Bill / Mad Zach Modulators tutorials (Live-12-stamped)
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `live-12-feature`, `modulators`, `envelope-follower`, `lfo`, `note-modulator`, `principle`

#### C2. `scale-aware-midi-and-global-scale`

- **Title:** Scale-Aware MIDI and the Global Scale (Live 12)
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/live-12
- **Subtopics:**
  - the project-wide Scale setting and per-clip override
  - how scale-aware MIDI devices respect the active scale
  - the Scale device (constrain notes to scale)
  - workflow implications (no more manual key-checking before quantize-pitch)
  - microtonal scale support and .ascl import (cross-link C4)
  - what this changes for users coming from Logic / Studio One scale features
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Scale and Key; Ableton Live 12 launch video
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `live-12-feature`, `scale-aware`, `midi`, `tuning`

#### C3. `midi-generators-and-transformations`

- **Title:** MIDI Generators and Transformations (Live 12)
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/live-12
- **Subtopics:**
  - the MIDI Tools sidebar (Generators and Transformations)
  - **Generators (verified):** Stacks, Rhythm, Seed, Shape, Euclidean
  - **Transformations (verified):** Arpeggiate (note: this is the transformation; the plan originally miscategorized "Arp" as a generator), Chop, Connect, Ornament, Quantize, Recombine, Span, Strum, Time Warp, Velocity Shaper
  - Live 12.1+ added Glissando and LFO MPE transformations
  - destructive vs. preview workflow
  - when to reach for these vs. the classic MIDI-effects chain (cross-link I11)
  - generative patterns the Generators make easy
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — MIDI Generators, MIDI Transformations; Ableton Live 12 launch video; Mr. Bill MIDI tools tutorials (Live-12-stamped)
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `live-12-feature`, `midi-generators`, `midi-transformations`, `generative`

#### C4. `tuning-systems-and-microtonal-support`

- **Title:** Tuning Systems and Microtonal Support (Live 12)
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/live-12
- **Subtopics:**
  - .scl/.ascl scale-file import
  - **per-project tuning** (NOT per-device — the original plan was wrong on this)
  - which stock instruments are tuning-aware (Wavetable, Drift, Meld, Operator — verify against manual)
  - common alternate tunings (Just Intonation, 19-TET, Bohlen-Pierce) and how to load them
  - workflow for microtonal MIDI composition in Live
  - limits (third-party plugins that ignore tuning, latency)
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Tuning Systems; Ableton tuning-systems walkthrough video
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `live-12-feature`, `tuning`, `microtonal`, `xenharmonic`

#### C5. `stem-separation`

- **Title:** Stem Separation in Live 12.3+
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/live-12
- **Subtopics:**
  - the browser-native Stem Separation feature (**Live 12.3+, November 25, 2025** — NOT 12.1 as the original plan said)
  - the four-stem output (vocals, drums, bass, other)
  - quality limits — what works well, what bleeds
  - workflow for sample-flipping a finished track into MIDI-ready stems
  - comparison to third-party (RipX, Spleeter) — when to reach for which
  - legal/usage notes (don't use for distribution-rights-infringing flips)
- **Live version:** Live 12.3+
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Stem Separation; Ableton 12.3 release notes (November 25, 2025)
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `live-12-feature`, `stem-separation`, `sampling`

#### C6. `push-3-standalone-workflow`

- **Title:** Push 3 Standalone Workflow
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/live-12
- **Subtopics:**
  - what Push 3 standalone is (a self-contained Live instance running on the Push hardware)
  - the browser and storage workflow
  - project portability between standalone Push and desktop Live
  - which devices and features are/aren't available in standalone (third-party plugin limits)
  - the workflow split — sketch on Push, finish on desktop
  - battery, latency, CPU realities
- **Live version:** Live 12.x + Push 3 standalone firmware (current)
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Push 3 documentation; Ableton Push 3 standalone walkthrough; Mad Zach Push 3 tutorials (version-stamped)

#### C7. `live-12-point-releases-changelog`

- **Title:** Live 12 Point Releases — What Each Version Added
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/live-12
- **Subtopics:**
  - Live 12.0 launch feature set (modulators, scale-aware, MIDI tools, tuning, Drift, Meld, Roar, Granulator III)
  - Live 12.1 additions (verify against release notes — stem separation, Note Modulator, etc.)
  - Live 12.2 additions (verify against release notes)
  - any later 12.x additions verified at time of writing
  - features still missing (commonly requested but not yet shipped) — only if verified by official roadmap or release notes
- **Live version:** Live 12.x (current point release at time of research)
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 release notes (every dot version); Ableton website "What's New in Live 12"
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `live-12-feature`, `changelog`, `reference-fact`

## D. Production Patterns — `corpus/daw/ableton/patterns/`

#### D1. `parallel-compression-in-live`

- **Title:** Parallel Compression in Ableton Live
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/patterns
- **Subtopics:**
  - the NY-drum / Motown parallel-comp concept
  - implementing via a Return track (send the drums, smash the return)
  - implementing via the Compressor's dry/wet knob (the Live shortcut)
  - parallel comp on drum bus, vocals, mix bus
  - typical Glue / Drum Buss settings for parallel use
  - blend levels and the "make it audibly worse, then dial back" rule
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Compressor (dry/wet); Mike Senior *Mixing Secrets*; SOS parallel-comp articles
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `production-pattern`, `parallel-compression`, `mixing`, `recipe`

#### D2. `sidechain-ducking-classic-vs-modulator`

- **Title:** Sidechain Ducking in Live — Classic Compressor vs Envelope Follower Modulator
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/patterns
- **Subtopics:**
  - the classic Compressor-sidechain ducking trick (kick → bass)
  - the Envelope Follower modulator approach (Live 12+) — drives any mapped parameter from an audio source
  - when each fits (Compressor for traditional ducking; Envelope Follower for non-compressor parameter ducking — filter cutoff, send level, reverb wet)
  - typical settings per genre (deep house, pop, rock)
  - cross-link to I8 Timeless classic-sidechain file for the pre-modulator workflow
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Compressor (sidechain), Envelope Follower; Attack Magazine sidechain articles
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `production-pattern`, `sidechain`, `envelope-follower`, `mixing`, `recipe`

#### D3. `vocal-chains-in-live`

- **Title:** Vocal Chains in Live — Stock-Device Recipes
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/patterns
- **Subtopics:**
  - the canonical chain order (HPF / EQ Eight → de-ess / Multiband Dynamics → Compressor → EQ Eight → Saturator → reverb send)
  - de-essing in Live without a dedicated de-esser (Multiband Dynamics on the 6–10 kHz band)
  - the two-comp trick (slow Glue then fast Compressor)
  - vocal automation via clip envelopes vs. track automation
  - vocal reverb sends (one short, one long, pre-delay matched to tempo)
  - cross-link to general-corpus `vocal-mixing.md`
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Multiband Dynamics, Compressor, EQ Eight; SOS Inside Track vocal-chain features
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `production-pattern`, `vocal-chain`, `de-esser`, `mixing`, `recipe`

- **Title:** Drum Bus Chains in Live
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/patterns
- **Subtopics:**
  - the Drum Buss + Glue Compressor combination
  - the parallel Drum Buss trick (Drum Buss on a Return, blend with dry)
  - Saturator vs. Roar on the drum bus
  - transient shaping (Drum Buss "Transients" knob)
  - low-end management (Drum Buss "Boom" vs. dedicated sub processing)
  - the simple drum-bus chain (one Glue, one EQ, one Saturator) as a starting point
- **Live version:** Live 12.x
- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Drum Buss, Glue Compressor; Mr. Bill drum-bus tutorials
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `production-pattern`, `drum-bus`, `drum-buss`, `mixing`, `recipe`

  - what belongs on the Master vs. on a "mix bus" Group track
  - the Glue + tilt-EQ + limiter chain as a default
  - sidechain-able mix-bus glue
  - the case for putting the master chain in early (mix-into-the-master) vs. late (master at the end)
  - Live's Limiter device vs. third-party
  - tonal-balance reference plugins in the master chain

## F. Practical Frictions — `corpus/daw/ableton/friction/`

#### F1. `silent-track-diagnostic-flow`

- **Title:** Why Is My Track Silent — A Diagnostic Flow
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/friction
- **Subtopics:**
  - the decision tree (monitoring state → arm state → input/output routing → mute/solo/folder mute → frozen → device disabled → AU/VST scan errors)
  - common Live-specific traps (Monitor=Off on a record-armed track during playback, group track muted at parent level, Cue Out routed wrong)
  - the "everything looks fine but nothing plays" case (audio device sample-rate mismatch, External Instrument latency)
  - how to use Live's CPU meter and Audio Preferences to triangulate
- **Live version:** Live 12.x

## H. Logic Parity Map — `corpus/daw/ableton/reference/`

#### H1. `ableton-to-logic-parity-map`

- **Title:** Ableton-to-Logic Parity Map
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/reference
- **Subtopics:**
  - per-concept table — Ableton feature, Logic Pro equivalent, notes
  - covered concepts: Session view, Arrangement view, Drum Rack, EQ Eight, Compressor, Glue, Hybrid Reverb, Delay, Modulators, Wavetable, Operator, follow actions, takes-lane comping, warp modes, freeze/flatten/bounce, sends/returns, Group tracks, Push integration
  - where Logic has *no direct equivalent* (Session view, Drum Rack chain-selectors, Modulators-as-track-devices) — flag explicitly
  - where Logic does the same thing differently (e.g., Flex Time vs. Warp modes; Track Stacks vs. Group tracks)
  - useful for ARLO when the user identifies as a Logic user instead

- **Canonical references:** Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual; Apple Logic Pro 11 User Guide

## I. Timeless Techniques — `corpus/daw/ableton/timeless/`

For every file in this section, the agent must verify the technique still works in Live 12 — cite both the original creator (with the Live version stamped on screen) AND a Live-12 verification source (manual section, or a current tutorial demonstrating the technique). When a Live-12 tool partially supersedes the technique, show both with notes on when to reach for which.

#### I1. `resampling-discipline`

- **Title:** Resampling Discipline — The Most-Used Producer Technique in Live
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/timeless
- **Subtopics:**
  - what resampling actually is (record into a new audio track, then manipulate as a new source)
  - the "Resampling" track input setting
  - committing a synth-plus-effects chain via resampling
  - resampling for chop-and-flip
  - the discipline of recording vs. just bouncing-in-place — when each fits
  - resampling as the basis for half-speed flips and reverse tricks
- **Live version:** Live 8+ (verified Live 12)
- **Canonical references:** Mad Zach / Slynk classic resampling tutorials (version-stamped); Ableton Live 12 Reference Manual — Routing (Resampling); Mr. Bill resampling deep-dives
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `daw-ableton-timeless`, `resampling`, `sampling`, `methodology`, `recipe`

- **Folder:** daw/ableton/timeless
- **Subtopics:**
  - the three Convert Audio to MIDI options (Drums, Melody, Harmony)
  - what each is good at and where each fails (Drums on percussive clips works well; Melody on solo vocal lines decent; Harmony unreliable)

  - extracting a chord progression from a reference song
  - cleanup after extraction (manual MIDI editing)
  - Live-9 origin, still bedrock in Live 12

  - the trap of looping forever (paralysis by infinite tweaking)
  - cross-link to general-corpus `finishing-work-and-completion.md`
- **Live version:** Live (methodology, not version-bound) (verified Live 12)
- **Canonical references:** Tom Cosm "8 Bars" tutorials; Ill Gates writings on session-view discipline; Dennis DeSantis *Making Music*
- **Tags:** `daw-ableton`, `live-12`, `daw-ableton-timeless`, `methodology`, `preproduction`, `session-view`, `principle`

#### I14. `bounce-in-place-commit-discipline`

- **Title:** Bounce in Place — The Commit Discipline
- **Scope:** daw
- **Folder:** daw/ableton/timeless
- **Subtopics:**
  - when to commit a sound (free CPU, lock the decision, mental closure)
  - when to keep flexibility (still searching for a sound, mix not finalized)
  - the producer-judgment heuristic ("commit on what you're sure of, hold what you're not")
  - the Freeze-then-Flatten old workflow vs. Bounce in Place (cross-link A6)
  - the "commit the synth, keep the FX" pattern
- **Live version:** Live 11+ for Bounce in Place; older workflows via Freeze/Flatten (verified Live 12)

  - Live's mixer vs. Pro Tools / Logic mixers — what's different

---

## Suggested firing order

If you don't want to fire all 62 prompts at once, this priority order builds the most useful retrieval surface first:

**Wave 1 (highest-leverage, ~14 files):**
A1, A3, A6, A7, B2, B3, B4, C1, C3, D2, D3, E2, I1, I9
*(Session/Arrangement, routing, freeze/bounce, racks, compressors, reverbs, delay, modulators, MIDI generators, sidechain, vocal chains, warp modes, resampling, parallel bus)*

**Wave 2 (depth fill, ~16 files):**
A2, A4, A5, B1, B5, B6, B7, B9, B10, B11, D1, D4, D5, D7, E1, E4
*(track types, recording, clip launching, EQ Eight, utilities, Saturator/Roar, Pedal/Amp/Cab, synths × 3, samplers, parallel comp, drum bus, mix bus, sound design, comping, slicing)*

**Wave 3 (Live-12-specific + frictions, ~12 files):**
C2, C4, C5, C6, C7, B8, D6, E3, F1, F2, F3, F4
*(scale-aware, tuning, stem separation, Push 3, changelog, spectral devices, mastering, warping discipline, silent-track, monitoring, latency, CPU)*

**Wave 4 (timeless + reference, ~20 files):**
F5, G1, H1, I2, I3, I4, I5, I6, I7, I8, I10, I11, I12, I13, I14, I15, I16, I17, I18, I19
*(everything else — timeless techniques, shortcuts, Logic parity, recovery)*

---

## Post-processing checklist

After each prompt returns markdown, run the same check from `PROMPTS.md` with these additions for the DAW stream:

- [ ] File saved at `corpus/daw/ableton/{area}/{filename}.md`
- [ ] Front-matter source line reads `Deep-research synthesis (2026-05) — Ableton Live 12.x`
- [ ] Every device or feature claim is inline-cited to the Live 12 Reference Manual or a version-stamped tutorial
- [ ] No pre-Live-12 feature claims retrofitted into Live 12 wording (Modulators, scale-aware, MIDI Generators, Drift, Meld, Roar, Auto Shift, Granulator III, stem separation — all Live 12+)
- [ ] Sources section has only real URLs (no hallucinated citations)
- [ ] `See also:` footer links existing general-corpus files where the DAW-agnostic concept lives
- [ ] Tags include `daw-ableton` and `live-12` at minimum
